title: Butterfly cakes
subtitle: Vanilla sponge buns filled with whipped cream
excerpt: Fairy-cake sponges with the top cut away, filled with whipped cream and jam, and the cut piece placed back as two wings dusted with icing sugar.
sourceNotes: Butterfly cakes are a variation on the fairy cake, distinguished only by the technique of cutting a cone from the top of each baked bun, splitting it in two, and replanting the halves in a cream filling. They are documented across British baking guides from the mid-twentieth century and appear in Marguerite Patten's Cookery in Colour (1960), Delia Smith's Complete Cookery Course (1978), and Mary Berry's Baking Bible (1994). The butterfly silhouette is achieved reliably only if the cream is piped or mounded high enough to support the wings at an upward angle.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the sponges up to a day ahead and keep in an airtight tin. Fill and decorate within 2 hours of serving.

baking.decoratingTechnique: whipped cream piped into the cavity; two wing pieces placed upright; icing sugar dusted over

content.text: The shape depends on getting the cream mound right. It needs to be tall enough that the two wing pieces lean outward at an angle rather than lying flat. Pipe the cream in a small mound in the centre of each cavity, then press the wings in while the cream is still soft enough to hold them.
Whip the cream to soft peaks only. Over-whipped cream becomes stiff and grainy and will not pipe cleanly. Stop when the cream holds a gentle shape that falls slowly from the whisk.

Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Line two 12-hole bun tins with paper cases. Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} until pale and fluffy. Beat in the {{eggs}} one at a time, then the {{vanilla-extract}}. Fold in the {{plain-flour}} and {{baking-powder}}. Place a heaped teaspoon of batter into each case. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes until risen and golden. Cool in the tin for 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack to cool completely.

Using a small knife, cut a shallow cone from the top of each cake (roughly the size of a 2p coin, about 1 cm deep). Cut each cone in half from tip to base: these are the wings. Spoon a small amount of {{jam}} into each cavity. Whip the {{double-cream}} to soft peaks. Pipe or spoon a mound of cream over the jam, filling and slightly overflowing the cavity. Press two wing halves into the cream at opposite angles, leaning outward. Dust generously with {{icing-sugar}} and serve within 2 hours.
